subtopics.title: History and Development of Computers
common_misconceptions: Charles Babbage invented the first computer (he designed the Analytical Engine but never built it)
ENIAC was the first computer (it was the first general-purpose electronic computer)
subtopics.teaching_note: Generations: 1st (1940-56): vacuum tubes, machine language, large size. 2nd (1956-63): transistors, assembly language. 3rd (1964-71): ICs, high-level languages. 4th (1971-present): microprocessors, VLSI, GUIs. 5th (emerging): AI, quantum computing. Key figures: Charles Babbage (father of computer), Ada Lovelace (first programmer), John von Neumann (stored program concept), Alan Turing (Turing machine). Nigerian context: NITDA, NCC promote ICT development.

subtopics.title: Types and Classification of Computers
common_misconceptions: Microcomputers are small in capability (they can be very powerful)
Mainframes are obsolete (still used by banks, airlines)
subtopics.teaching_note: Classification by size/capability: Supercomputer (fastest, weather forecasting, nuclear research), Mainframe (large organizations, banks), Minicomputer (mid-size businesses), Microcomputer (PCs, laptops, tablets). By type: Analog (continuous data, e.g., speedometer), Digital (discrete data, binary), Hybrid (combination). By purpose: General purpose, Special purpose, Embedded (in devices: washing machines, cars, ATMs).

subtopics.title: Components of Computer Hardware
common_misconceptions: CPU is the whole computer case (NO, it's a chip inside)
RAM and ROM are the same
More storage means faster computer (storage ≠ memory)
subtopics.teaching_note: CPU = ALU (Arithmetic Logic Unit - calculations) + CU (Control Unit - coordinates) + Registers (fast temporary storage). Memory: RAM (volatile, read-write, measured in GB) and ROM (non-volatile, read-only, stores BIOS). Storage: HDD (magnetic, spinning), SSD (flash memory, faster), optical (CD/DVD), USB flash drives. Von Neumann architecture: stored program concept - instructions and data in same memory.

subtopics.title: Number Systems and Data Representation
common_misconceptions: Binary is only used in computers
subtopics.teaching_note: Binary (base 2): uses 0,1. Each digit = bit. 8 bits = 1 byte. Binary to decimal: sum of powers of 2. Conversion: Decimal to binary - repeated division by 2. Binary to hex - group in 4s. Data units: 1 KB = 1024 B, 1 MB = 1024 KB, 1 GB = 1024 MB, 1 TB = 1024 GB. ASCII: 7-bit code, 128 characters. Unicode: 16-bit, supports multiple languages including Igbo, Yoruba, Hausa characters.

subtopics.title: Programming Concepts
common_misconceptions: All programming languages are the same
A program without errors always produces correct output (logic errors exist)
subtopics.teaching_note: Algorithm: step-by-step solution to a problem. Properties: finite, definite, input, output, effective. Flowchart symbols: oval (start/end), rectangle (process), diamond (decision), parallelogram (input/output). Pseudocode: English-like description. Programming language levels: Machine code (binary), Assembly (mnemonics), High-level (Python, Java, C++). Translators: Compiler (converts all at once), Interpreter (line by line), Assembler (assembly to machine code). BASIC was traditionally taught in Nigerian schools; Python is increasingly popular.
